data.narrative:
Quandela is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Valerian Giesz, Pascale Senellart, and Niccolo Somaschi, backed by PROQCIMA. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identified peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable data at Quandela's stage and tier. The quantum computing peer universe contains only one same-stage comparable, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—falling below the three-datapoint minimum required for robust median analysis. Broadening the filter to the full seven-company same-sector cohort would introduce substantial stage and business-model heterogeneity, rendering cross-sectional multiples misleading rather than informative.

The analysis encountered a fundamental data constraint: private Series B quantum computing companies with disclosed valuations remain sparse, and public quantum peers operate at different scales and development phases. Without sufficient triangulated comparables, peer-based valuation methods cannot be credibly applied. A complete valuation would require either (1) bottom-up discounted cash flow modeling with explicit technology risk assumptions, (2) precedent transaction analysis if M&A data exists in the sector, or (3) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent quantum or deep-tech Series B cohorts—each introducing different methodological assumptions. The current dataset supports only negative inference: traditional multiple-based approaches lack the empirical foundation for point-estimate valuation.
